name: Abkhaz

sources.author: Hewitt, B. George
sources.title: Abkhaz (Lingua Descriptive Studies)
sources.notes: Reference grammar; the source to use when filling the inventory.

notes: Abkhaz (аԥсшәа) — Northwest Caucasian language of Abkhazia, written in an extended Cyrillic alphabet since 1954. THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED in this spec (graphemes/allophones are empty). The alphabet has 62+ letters and digraphs encoding a very large consonant inventory (~58 consonants, only 2 phonemic vowels /a ə/), with three-way plain/labialised/palatalised series and productive ejectives; a reliable letter→IPA table needs research against a reference grammar (Hewitt 1979) before it can be claimed here.
